criterion: The Agent identifies that Acme Cloud's currently-posted Q1 2026 sales-tax journal entry (je_b5e31cb22eb246d5, entry_number 'JE-acme_cloud-acme_cloud_FP-2026-03-0076') was posted on 2026-03-31 in acme_cloud_FP-2026-03 with total debits and credits of $42,180.55, prepared by Tom Chang and approved by Edith Banda.
justification: The prompt instructs Hannah to 'walk through what's currently sitting on Acme's books for Q1 sales tax' and reconcile against the memo. Identifying the specific posted JE (its ID, period, posting date, total, and preparer) is the anchor of that walk-through; without it, the reconciliation has no fixed point. The $42,180.55 total is the figure the agent must contrast against the memo's $40,364.55 position.
evidence: In the Agent's final response, look for je_b5e31cb22eb246d5 (or the entry_number JE-acme_cloud-acme_cloud_FP-2026-03-0076), the $42,180.55 total, the 2026-03-31 posting date in acme_cloud_FP-2026-03, and Tom Chang as preparer.

criterion: The Agent identifies that the SaaS-taxability determination memo defends a four-state Q1 2026 position covering TX, NY, WA, and AZ with a grand total of $40,364.55 (TX $24,492.10, NY $8,944.25, WA $2,118.20, AZ $4,810.00).
justification: The prompt centers on reconciling the books to 'the position the memo defends'. Hannah must surface the memo's defended position to make the reconciliation legible to herself and Ming for Monday's sign-off. The four states and the $40,364.55 grand total are the load-bearing facts of the determination.
evidence: In the Agent's final response, look for the four states (TX, NY, WA, AZ), the $40,364.55 total, and the per-state amounts $24,492.10 / $8,944.25 / $2,118.20 / $4,810.00 (or close approximations to each).

criterion: The Agent identifies that the posted Q1 journal entry includes GA ($9,844.15) and NC ($7,760.10) which the determination memo concludes are SaaS-exempt or services-not-taxed and therefore should be reversed.
justification: The prompt says the determination 'pulled a couple of states out of scope that we'd been treating as in', which is precisely GA and NC on this entry. Reporting these state amounts back to Hannah is core to the reconciliation, since these are the line items that drive the correction.
evidence: In the Agent's final response, look for GA $9,844.15 and NC $7,760.10 being called out as exempt per the memo and identified as components of the posted JE that must be reversed.

criterion: The Agent identifies that the posted Q1 entry's business justification assumed a CA standing-accrual offset of approximately $29,000 that does not exist (the CA-only standing entries from prior periods were reclassified after the prior-period review, so the full Q1 liability should be a new incremental accrual rather than incremental-over-CA).
justification: The 2026-04-08 Slack thread is where Hannah explicitly stated 'there is no $29K sitting in 225000 from earlier months. Treat the full Q1 liability as a new incremental accrual.' Catching this is the difference between a numerically clean reconciliation and a quietly-broken one; the agent has to read the channel back-and-forth Hannah refers to in the prompt and surface that the phantom offset is part of why the books don't match the memo.
evidence: In the Agent's final response, look for a statement that the approximately $29,000 CA offset/standing accrual referenced in the posted JE's justification does not actually exist on the books (CA standing entries were reclassified in prior-period review).

criterion: The Agent identifies that account 525000 (the debit side of the posted Q1 sales-tax JE) does not exist on Acme Cloud's chart of accounts.
justification: The prompt explicitly warns 'there's no clean Sales Tax Payable account on Acme so the coding takes thought.' The agent must surface that the original JE was posted to a phantom expense account on top of the other reconciliation issues, since silently re-posting to the same phantom account on the correction would propagate the error.
evidence: In the Agent's final response, look for a statement that account 525000 does not exist on Acme Cloud (acme_cloud), verified by listing the entity's chart of accounts.

criterion: The Agent identifies that account 225000 on Acme Cloud is 'Accrued Salaries & Bonuses' (a payroll accrual), not a sales-tax-payable account, and that no dedicated Sales Tax Payable account exists on Acme Cloud's chart of accounts.
justification: Continuing the CoA-gap thread from the prompt: the credit side of the original JE landed in the payroll-accrual account, which is the second half of why 'the coding takes thought'. The agent has to surface this misclassification so Hannah and Ming can make an informed call (open a dedicated Sales Tax Payable account vs. interim-code through an accrued-professional-services slot).
evidence: In the Agent's final response, look for a statement that account 225000 on Acme is the Accrued Salaries & Bonuses / payroll accrual account and that Acme has no dedicated Sales Tax Payable account.

criterion: The Agent identifies that acme_cloud_FP-2026-03 (the original posting period) is closed and that acme_cloud_FP-2026-05 is the open period the corrective entry will land in.
justification: The prompt explicitly says 'the period the original entry hit is already closed, so anything corrective lands in a sensible open one.' Calling out which period is open and explaining the routing decision is required for Hannah to understand and approve the proposal.
evidence: In the Agent's final response, look for a statement that FP-2026-03 (the original period) is closed and FP-2026-05 is the open Acme period selected for the correction.

criterion: The Agent creates a corrective journal entry on Acme Cloud in period acme_cloud_FP-2026-05, in draft status, with entry_type 'adjusting' and is_standard_entry false.
justification: This is the central write action of the task; the prompt asks the agent to 'get the correction teed up' and explicitly says to land it in a sensible open period and leave it in a state Hannah can review before Monday sign-off. Drafting (not submitting/approving/posting) is required because Hannah and Ming have not yet signed off. Adjusting + non-standard reflects the one-off restatement character of the entry.
evidence: In the trajectory, look for a journal-entry creation on acme_cloud with period_id acme_cloud_FP-2026-05, status 'draft', entry_type 'adjusting', and is_standard_entry false.

criterion: The Agent's corrective journal entry description or business justification references reversal/restatement of the original Q1 sales-tax entry by ID (je_b5e31cb22eb246d5) or by entry number (JE-acme_cloud-acme_cloud_FP-2026-03-0076).
justification: The journal-entry creation tool does not expose a reverses_entry_id parameter, so the explicit linkage to the original entry has to live in the description or business justification text. Without that linkage the audit trail breaks; anyone reading the JE later cannot tie it to what it corrects.
evidence: In the trajectory, inspect the corrective JE's description and business_justification fields for a reference to je_b5e31cb22eb246d5 or the entry number JE-acme_cloud-acme_cloud_FP-2026-03-0076.

criterion: The Agent's corrective journal entry description or business justification references the four-state defended position totaling $40,364.55, naming TX $24,492.10, NY $8,944.25, WA $2,118.20, and AZ $4,810.00 (or close approximations).
justification: The corrective entry's purpose is to restate Acme's Q1 liability to the position the determination memo defends. The defended position itself (total and per-state composition) has to live on the corrective entry so a reviewer can verify the restatement amount without going back to the memo.
evidence: In the trajectory, inspect the corrective JE's description and business_justification fields for $40,364.55 and the four state-level amounts (TX $24,492.10, NY $8,944.25, WA $2,118.20, AZ $4,810.00).

criterion: The Agent's corrective journal entry business justification surfaces the Acme chart-of-accounts gap (account 525000 does not exist on Acme and account 225000 is the payroll accrual rather than a sales-tax-payable account), and flags the open coding question for Ming.
justification: The prompt explicitly tells the agent to 'mind the chart' because Acme has no clean Sales Tax Payable account. Surfacing the gap inside the corrective entry's justification (rather than silently re-posting to another wrong account) preserves Hannah and Ming's ability to decide on the right coding before sign-off.
evidence: In the trajectory, inspect the corrective JE's business_justification field for callouts that account 525000 does not exist on Acme, that account 225000 is the payroll/salary accrual, and an explicit open question or recommendation to Ming on the correct coding.

criterion: The Agent uploads a document to Records Vault attached to the Acme Cloud client engagement with retention_policy_code 'IRS_TAX_7Y'.
justification: The prompt explicitly instructs the agent to 'put the correction trail into the engagement file under the same retention as the determination so the audit cycle has it cleanly'; the determination memo carries IRS_TAX_7Y, so the correction trail must mirror exactly that retention code (not AICPA_SQMS_7Y, not FIRM_INTERNAL).
evidence: In the trajectory, look for a Records Vault upload with related_resource_id 'acme_cloud' (or related_resource_type 'client_engagement' tied to Acme) and retention_policy_code 'IRS_TAX_7Y'.

criterion: The Agent's Records Vault correction-trail document summarizes the reconciliation between the posted Q1 sales-tax JE and the memo's four-state position, references the draft corrective entry in acme_cloud_FP-2026-05, and documents the chart-of-accounts gap and the open coding question for Ming.
justification: The audit cycle needs the trail to be self-contained; anyone pulling the file later (Ming, the external auditor, a future cycle reviewer) should see the original JE, the memo position, the proposed corrective JE, and the open CoA question without having to chase across systems. The prompt scopes the trail to exactly this content.
evidence: In the trajectory, inspect the uploaded document's title and content for references to the posted Q1 JE total ($42,180.55), the memo's $40,364.55 four-state position, the draft corrective entry in FP-2026-05, and the CoA gap (account 525000/225000) along with the open question for Ming.

criterion: The Agent creates a Linear issue in the Tax team under the Quarterly Tax Deadline Readiness project (proj_qtr_tax_deadlines / team_tax).
justification: The prompt explicitly says 'if this really has been sitting open since Q1 close, log a follow-up on the tax-deadline readiness side so we catch this handoff gap on the next cycle.' The deadline-readiness side maps to the Quarterly Tax Deadline Readiness project on the tax team in Linear.
evidence: In the trajectory, look for a Linear issue creation with team 'team_tax' and project 'proj_qtr_tax_deadlines' (Quarterly Tax Deadline Readiness).

criterion: The Agent's Linear issue describes the cycle-handoff gap (the Q1 sales-tax accrual posted before the determination memo was filed and reconciled on Slack, leaving the books out of step with the defended position), and proposes a pre-quarter-close checkpoint that confirms the taxability determination is finalized before any sales-tax JE posts on the next cycle.
justification: The follow-up only catches the handoff gap on the next cycle if the issue actually describes the gap and proposes a control. A bare 'follow up on this' ticket would not prevent the same misalignment from repeating, which is exactly what Hannah is asking the issue to prevent.
evidence: In the trajectory, inspect the Linear issue title and description for a description of the timing gap (Q1 JE posted before the determination memo / Slack reconciliation) and a proposed pre-quarter-close gate or checkpoint.

criterion: The Agent sends Tom Chang (tom.chang@brookfieldcpas.com) a heads-up about the Q1 sales-tax reconciliation, by either email or direct message.
justification: The prompt explicitly says 'Give Tom a heads-up either way. He carried the quarter and should sanity-check what you find against the position he filed.' The channel is not specified, so an email to tom.chang@brookfieldcpas.com or a direct message to Tom both satisfy the ask.
evidence: In the trajectory, look for an email with tom.chang@brookfieldcpas.com as a recipient, or a direct message addressed to Tom Chang, referencing the Acme Q1 sales-tax reconciliation.

criterion: The Agent's heads-up to Tom Chang summarizes the discrepancy between the posted Q1 accrual ($42,180.55) and the memo's defended position ($40,364.55), references the GA/NC/CA-offset composition issue, and points Tom at the draft corrective journal entry in acme_cloud_FP-2026-05.
justification: A heads-up that Tom can 'sanity-check what you find against the position he filed' needs the two numbers (posted vs defended), the substantive reason they differ (GA/NC reversal + missing CA offset), and a pointer to where the proposed correction lives. Anything thinner doesn't let Tom actually sanity-check.
evidence: In the trajectory, inspect the email or direct message to Tom Chang for the $42,180.55 posted figure, the $40,364.55 memo position, mention of the GA/NC exempt states (or the CA standing-accrual offset issue), and a reference to the draft corrective JE in acme_cloud_FP-2026-05.
